返回本批次

黑洞我的电子邮件

Blackholing My Email

www.johnsto.co.uk 作者 semyonsh 分数 168 评论 35 2026/04/07 16:42
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 根据我自己的经验，您发现自己要求电子邮件提供商屏蔽您的主要个人电子邮件地址的情况并不特别常见。但在 2002 年，一系列独特的世纪之交事件迫使我这样做，以防止我们的宽带帐户被终止。当时，我是 dv@btinternet.com 的所有者 - 对此感到自豪，因为“Dave”、“DaveJ”和我的名字/姓氏的所有其他可接受的变体似乎已被我们 ISP 的其他订阅者占用。找到一个与“Dave”有点相似的可用的 2 个字母的用户名感觉很幸运。由于高传播性电子邮件蠕虫的出现，2000 年代初期的电子邮件面临着挑战。也许最著名的是，“ILOVEYOU”蠕虫病毒赢得了国际关注，由于这种破坏性病毒会删除文件并将其自身通过电子邮件发送给恰好出现在任何不幸的收件人（尽管使用 Outlook）地址列表中的每个人，因此数千个计算机网络、学校、企业和社区被瘫痪（在某些情况下被摧毁）。即使在 2000 年，这个数字也有数百万人。到 2002 年，这些蠕虫病毒已经进化得变得更加复杂和狡猾，因为它们的作者试图传播得更广、更快。最具破坏性的变化之一 - 至少对于我可怜的收件箱来说 - 是能够不仅从用户地址簿中获取潜在目标的电子邮件地址，还可以从用户硬盘上的任何类似文本的文件中获取潜在目标的电子邮件地址。 此时，我正在使用我的第四张官方 CS 地图 - Dust 2 - 这意味着在每次 CS 安装中至少有 4 次提到我的个人电子邮件地址。每当有人连接到运行我的地图的服务器时，就会弹出一个电子邮件地址，作为任务简报的一部分：灰尘 - 炸弹/拆除，由 DaveJ (dv@btinternet.com) 纹理由 Macman (MacManInfi@aol.com) 提供
  2. 反恐分子：防止恐怖分子轰炸化学武器箱。团队成员必须拆除威胁目标区域的任何炸弹。
  3. 恐怖分子：携带 C4 的恐怖分子必须销毁其中一处化学武器储藏处。
  4. 其他说明：任务中有 2 个化学武器储藏处。
  5. （按 FIRE 继续）这是 de_dust.txt - Dust 2 和 Cobble 的等效项，加上整个 readme.txt。所有 CS 贡献者的电子邮件都列在当时最流行的多人 FPS 的每次安装中，该游戏主要安装在连接互联网的计算机上，而当时的电子邮件蠕虫病毒专门通过互联网传播。您知道这是怎么回事……我的 dv@btinternet.com 帐户很快开始每天收到数十封甚至数百封来自蠕虫（如“Klez”）的电子邮件 - 数量如此之多，以至于 Outlook（谢天谢地，我的 Outlook 已打补丁）再也无法接收了。不再加载我的收件箱。我必须使用专用的 POP3 工具，它可以让我提前快速扫描并删除有问题的电子邮件，让合法邮件通过。幸运的是，可垃圾邮件很容易通过主题行中的常见模式识别出来，因此每天只需要几分钟就可以恢复收件箱访问。可惜，这种情况并没有持续多久。不久之后，我每天收到的几百封电子邮件变成了每天数千封电子邮件，这些电子邮件轻松地占据了我们的 ISP 慷慨地授予我当时慷慨的 15MB（兆字节！）邮箱空间。最糟糕的是，蠕虫作者在他们的电子邮件结构上变得更有创意，以逃避批量识别和删除，利用随机构建的主题行、正文、伪造的标题、被盗的文件附件，最令人烦恼的是，使用欺骗性的发件人地址，再次使用从他们遇到的任何地址簿和文件中收集的数据。我的父亲（实际帐户持有者）开始收到有关电子邮件使用的警告。我们的宽带账户面临被关闭的风险。游戏结束了。我宝贵的 2 个字母的电子邮件地址无法使用 - 所有有效的电子邮件在所有受感染的电子邮件中都完全丢失了，我的收件箱太满而无法接收新电子邮件，甚至许多确实通过的有效电子邮件都指控我向他们发送了蠕虫病毒（还记得那些欺骗性的标头吗？）我别无选择，只能要求 BT 客户服务 blackhole dv@btinternet.com 来保存我们的帐户，保护他们的电子邮件服务，并防止制造一场风暴那些可怜的人的电子邮件被意外退回，他们的地址被随机选择为欺骗性的“发件人”地址。24 年后，我父亲的 BT 互联网帐户仍然存在。出于好奇，我尝试登录并查看是否可以找到 dv@btinternet.com 的当前状态 - 但该功能几年前已被删除，并且无法再创建新的收件箱。我尝试向它发送一封电子邮件 - 并收到定期的“用户未知”回复，表明在某个时候黑洞已被删除，并且该帐户根本不存在。我很想知道这些年来它是否仍然收到任何电子邮件蠕虫。

Original Summary

  1. In my own experience, it’s not particularly often that you find yourself asking your email provider to blackhole your primary, personal email address. But in 2002, a unique series of turn-of-the-millennium events had me doing exactly that to prevent our broadband account from being terminated.Back then, I was the owner of dv@btinternet.com - proudly so, as all other acceptable variations of “Dave”, “DaveJ”, and my firstname/surname appeared to have been taken by other subscribers to our ISP. To find an available 2-letter username that was vaguely similar to “Dave” felt lucky.The early 2000s were challenging for email, thanks to the emergence of highly-transmissable email worms. Perhaps most famously, the “ILOVEYOU” worm earned international attention as thousands of computer networks, schools, businesses and communities were brought down - in some cases destroyed - by virtue of this destructive virus deleting files and emailing itself to everyone who happened to be in the address list of any unfortunate (albeit Outlook-using) recipient. Even in 2000, that was millions upon millions of people.By 2002 these worms had evolved to become considerably more sophisticated - and sneaky - as their authors attempted to spread wider and faster. One of the most devastating changes - at least for my poor, poor inbox - was the ability to source email addresses of potential targets not just from the users address book, but from any and every text-like file on the user’s hard disk.At this point I was onto my fourth official CS map - Dust 2 - which meant at least 4 mentions of my personal email address in every CS installation. An email address that would pop up every time someone connected to a server running one of my maps, as part of the mission briefing:Dust - Bomb/Defuse by DaveJ (dv@btinternet.com) textures by Macman (MacManInfi@aol.com)
  2. Counter-Terrorists: Prevent Terrorists from bombing chemical weapon crates. Team members must defuse any bombs that threaten targeted areas.
  3. Terrorists: The Terrorist carrying the C4 must destroy one of the chemical weapon stashes.
  4. Other Notes: There are 2 chemical weapon stashes in the mission.
  5. (Press FIRE to continue) This was de_dust.txt - equivalents existed for Dust 2 and Cobble, plus the overall readme.txt. All contributors to CS had their email listed somewhere in every single installation of the most popular multiplayer FPS of the time, a title primarily installed onto internet-connected computers, during a period of highly virulent email worms that spread exclusively via the internet.You see where this is going…My dv@btinternet.com account soon started receiving tens, and then hundreds, of emails a day from worms such as “Klez” - so many that Outlook (mine was patched, thankfully) could no longer load my inbox anymore. I had to use a dedicated POP3 tool that would let me rapidly scan and remove troublesome emails beforehand to let the legitimate ones through. Mercifully, trashable emails were easily identified by common patterns in the subject line, so this only took a few minutes a day to restore inbox access.Alas, this didn’t last.Before long those few hundred emails a day I was receiving became thousands of emails a day, which easily occupied the entirety of the at-the-time generous 15MB (megabytes!) of mailbox space so gracefully bestowed to me by BT, our ISP. Worst still, worm authors had become somewhat more creative with their email structure to evade bulk identification and removal, utilising randomly-constructed subject lines, bodies, feigned headers, stolen file attachments, and - most annoyingly - using spoofed sender addresses, again using harvested data from whatever address books and files they came across.My dad (the actual account holder) started to receive warnings about email usage. Our broadband account faced the risk of closure.The game was up. My precious 2-letter email address was unusable - any valid emails were being completely lost amongst all the infected ones, my inbox was too full to receive new emails, and even many of the valid ones that did get through were accusations of me sending them a worm (remember those spoofed headers?) I had little choice but to ask BT Customer Service to blackhole dv@btinternet.com to save our account, to protect their email service, and to prevent creating a storm of unexpected bounced emails to the poor souls whose addresses had been randomly chosen as the spoofed “from” address.24 years later, and my dad’s BT Internet account is still around. Out of curiosity, I tried to log in and see if I could find the current state of dv@btinternet.com - but that functionality was removed some years ago, and it’s not possible to create new inboxes anymore. I tried sending an email to it - and got a regular “User unknown” reply back, indicating that at some point the blackhole was removed, and the account simply became non-existent.I’d be curious to know if it is still receiving any email worms all these years later.

评论（默认中文）

3form 2026/04/07 21:36
对我来说，这篇文章的作者是 de_dust2 的作者这一事实才是真正的亮点！对于那些不知道的人来说，这是《反恐精英》中最受欢迎的地图，我希望它至今仍然如此。

这很有趣，因为我一直以为这些东西是由古老的互联网上的某个无名作者制作的，并且从来没有费心去检查和查找。更不用说，我期望通过该作者的随机博客文章偶然发现它，它甚至不是主要主题。

To me, the fact that the author of the article is the author of de_dust2 is the real highlight! For those who don't know, it's the most popular map ever in Counter Strike, and I expect so it remains to this day.

This is funny, as I always imagined these things to be made by some nameless author of good old Internet, and never bothered to check and look it up. Further less I expected to stumble upon it by said author's random blogpost where it's not even the primary topic.

在 HN 中查看
pete5x5 2026/04/07 22:09
在我看来，Dust2（以及提到的另一张地图 Cobble）既是艺术又是天才；我们大多数人永远不会做出任何能给这么多人带来欢乐的东西。

它激励我从事我热爱的事情，只是为了好玩。 你永远不知道会发生什么！

Dust2 (and Cobble, another map mentioned) is, IMO, both art and genius; most of us will never make anything that brings joy to so many.

It inspires me to work on things that I'm passionate about just for fun. You never know what might come out of it!

考虑到它被移植了多少其他游戏，它可能是所有视频游戏中最著名的地图
Likely the most well known map in all of video gaming, considering to how many other games it was ported

I had seen noclip's documentary about de_dust2 featuring him before but didn't piece the name together. Very happy to find that he has a blog!

我喜欢你的工作！感谢 de_dust2 的出色工作！
I love your work! Thanks for the huuuuge work on de_dust2!
在 HN 中查看
timrogers 2026/04/07 23:57
这是一张令人难以置信的地图 - 充满了许多有趣的回忆！
It’s an incredible map - so many fun memories!
在 HN 中查看
dmd 2026/04/07 23:13
我总是对 GMail 的垃圾邮件过滤能力感到惊讶，至少对我来说是这样。自 1996 年以来，我一直使用同一个电子邮件地址（距今已有 30 年了），并且在发布该地址时完全没有考虑到垃圾邮件防护。

我每天收到约 1000 封垃圾邮件。大约 1-2 个最终会进入收件箱。我经常会检查垃圾邮件，虽然我可能错过了一些东西，但我通常每个月发现的误报不到 1 个，而且从来都不是特别重要的事情。

I'm always stunned at how good GMail's spam filtering is, at least for me. I've been using the same email address since 1996 - that's 30 years now - and posting it with absolutely no thought to spam protection all over the place.

I get ~1000 spams per day. About 1-2 end up in inbox. Every so often I do go through my spam, and while it's possible I've missed something, I generally find less than 1 false positive a month and it's never anything especially important.

在 HN 中查看
WorldMaker 2026/04/08 01:07
我开始怀疑 GMail 的垃圾邮件文件夹是否在营销而不是实质内容。我使用同一个主要电子邮件地址的时间几乎相同数十年。我只有在垃圾邮件文件夹托管在 Google Workspace 上时才看到“最多”的垃圾邮件。实际上，试图浏览这些“每天 1000 条”时，其中很多人似乎都以奇怪的方式受到怀疑（为什么这甚至被传递到任何地方？），其中一些甚至看起来像谷歌只是将合法搜索广告中的随机广告副本转储到文件夹中。

（此外，它还说明了目前我每日垃圾邮件的两个最大来源是 Google 日历通知和随机 Firebase 帐户。这两个进一步让我怀疑 Google 的垃圾邮件过滤方法是否真诚。）

I've started to question if GMail's spam folder is marketing more than substance. I've used the same primary email address for nearly the same number of decades. The time I saw the "most" spam in a spam folder was only while it was hosted on Google Workspace. Actually trying to skim through those "1000s per day" a lot of them seemed suspect in strange ways (why was this even delivered anywhere?) and some of them even seemed like Google just dumping random ad copy from legitimate search ads into the folder.

(Also it says a lot that right now my two biggest sources of daily spam are Google Calendar Notifications and Random Firebase Accounts. Both of those further leave me questioning if Google's approach to spam filtering is sincere.)

在 HN 中查看
dhosek 2026/04/08 01:56
自从 Gmail 地址首次公布以来，我就一直使用它。它还会从我曾经自行托管的另一个古老的电子邮件地址转发电子邮件，并且仍然偶尔会收到真实的邮件。大多数垃圾邮件都发送到该其他地址。

刚刚看了一下我的垃圾邮件文件夹：自 3 月 18 日起有 207 封邮件，两个误报（均来自邮件列表），但没什么重要的。我想也许我在收件箱中的所有帐户中都看到了一封垃圾邮件。他们的过滤器受益于大量培训师的数据。

（顺便说一句，我要指出的是，我在我维护的一些网站上裸露发布的一些新地址很少收到垃圾邮件（同一时间范围内两个帐户之间有 14 条消息，其中大部分来自单个发件人，他决定向我发送他们的新闻稿，而无需任何选择退出的方式。）

I’ve had the same gmail address since it was first announced. It also gets email forwarded to it from another ancient email address that I used to self-host and that still gets occasional real mails. Most of the spam is addressed to that other address.

Just took a peek at my spam folder: 207 messages going back to March 18th, two false positives (both from mailing lists), but nothing critical. I think maybe I’ve seen one spam message across all my accounts in my inbox. Their filters benefit from a huge set of trainers on their data.

(As an aside, I would note that some newer addresses that I publish naked on some websites that I maintain get very little spam (14 messages between the two accounts in the same timeframe, most of which are from a single sender who decided that they should send me their press releases without any means of opting out.)

在 HN 中查看
nozzlegear 2026/04/07 23:22
我多年来一直是 Fastmail 的客户，并且对他们的垃圾邮件过滤也非常满意。任何通过的邮件要么获取自定义规则将其发送到影子领域，要么发送到我设置的特殊“学习垃圾邮件”文件夹，该文件夹将针对该邮件训练垃圾邮件过滤器。
I've been a Fastmail customer for years and have been pretty happy with their spam filtering too. Anything that does get through either gets a custom rule to send it to the shadow realm, or gets sent to a special "Learn spam" folder that I set up which will train the spam filter on that message.
在 HN 中查看
simgoh 2026/04/08 00:28
您是否注意到，随着时间的推移，发送到“学习垃圾邮件”文件夹的邮件数量会减少？我是一个相对较新的 Fastmail 客户 - 我为一些家庭帐户设置了一个域，我可以代表我年迈的家庭成员进行管理，因此它还没有收到大量电子邮件，但我预计将来会收到。
Have you noticed decreases over time by sending thigns to the "Learn Spam" folder? I'm a relatively new Fastmail customer - I setup a domain for some family accounts that I can manage on behalf of my aging family members so it's not receiving a lot of email _yet_ but I expect it to in the future.
在 HN 中查看
nozzlegear 2026/04/08 02:35
我肯定已经注意到了。默认垃圾邮件过滤器会捕获大部分我认为是垃圾邮件的内容，但对于那些能够通过的内容，需要“学习垃圾邮件”功能，因为它们 看 合法的。例如，我收到很多奇怪的“你是美国人，我来自[中国、印度等]，如果你参加所有面试/会议，然后让我做所有工作，我们可以赚很多钱”之类的电子邮件。它们看起来像正常的信件（也许是），所以它们偶尔会出现在我的收件箱中。当他们这样做时，我将它们发送到“学习垃圾邮件”文件夹，下次我检查实际的垃圾邮件文件夹时，我会发现 Fastmail 捕获了更多类似的邮件并将它们直接发送到那里。
I have noticed it for sure. The default spam filter catches most of what I'd consider spam, but the Learn Spam feature is needed for things that get through because they look legitimate. For example, I get a lot of those weird "You're an American, I'm from [China, India, etc.], we can make a lot of money if you go to all the interviews/meetings and then let me do all the work" kind of emails. They look like normal correspondence (maybe they are) so they occasionally end up in my inbox. When they do, I send them to the "Learn Spam" folder, and the next time I check my actual Spam folder I'll find that Fastmail caught several more just like it and sent them straight there.
在 HN 中查看
encom 2026/04/08 04:51
这里也是 Fastmail，我的电子邮件地址比 Gmail 更旧，而且可能比 HN 海报的很大一部分更旧。 Fastmails 垃圾邮件过滤器正常工作。我每个月都会收到一些假阴性，有些月份为零。我已将其设置为 /dev/null 最明显的垃圾邮件，并且我不记得最后一个误报。这种情况确实发生过，但极其罕见。谷歌垃圾邮件过滤器并不独特或神奇。

我从来不喜欢 Gmail 这个客户端。它不像 Outlook 那样糟糕，甚至没有什么可以与之媲美，但它总是妨碍我，并且以奇怪的 Googly 方式做事。我会继续使用 Kmail，谢谢。

Fastmail here too, and my email address is older than Gmail, and probably older than a significant portion of HN posters. Fastmails spam filter just works. I get a few false negatives per month, and some months zero. I've set it to /dev/null the most obvious spam, and I can't recall the last false positive. It's happened, but extremely rarely. Google spam filter is not unique or magical.

And I never liked Gmail the client. It's not as godawful as Outlook, nothing even comes close, but it always gets in my way and does things in weird Googly ways. I'll stick with Kmail, thanks.

在 HN 中查看
dhosek 2026/04/08 01:46
噢，天哪，ILOVEYOU 蠕虫——我记得从一位前同事那里得到了这个（我很确定他不是暗恋我），我问他从谁那里得到的，他打开了附件，他羞涩地认出了一位女同事，结果她有兴趣和我约会，但我当时已经在和某人约会了。我回顾了 1999 年至 2000 年的情况，当时我们对世界非常信任。
Oh man, the ILOVEYOU worm—I remember getting that from a former co-worker (who I was pretty sure was not secretly in love with me) and asking him who he got it from that he opened the attachment and he sheepishly identified a female co-worker who as it turned out had been interested in dating me but I was already dating someone at the time. I look back at how stuff was set up in 1999–2000 and man, we were so trusting of the world then.
在 HN 中查看
freediddy 2026/04/07 23:16
除尘……真是美好的时光！一张设计完美的地图，每个人都知道阻塞点是什么，最好的策略是什么，但势均力敌的对手之间的结果却永远无法保证。这就是完美的竞争环境！

最近，我的大孩子和他的朋友们通过 Steam 迷上了 CS2。我正在考虑举办一场“爸爸与孩子”锦标赛，因为我们正处于这样一个阶段，所有的爸爸都玩过 CS2，现在一些孩子已经足够大了，也足够优秀了，能够具有竞争力。

de_dust... such good times! A perfectly designed map where everyone knew what the chokepoints were and what the best strategies were but the outcomes between equal opponents was never guaranteed. That's what makes a perfect playing field!

I recently got my older kid and his friends hooked on CS2 via steam. I'm considering having a "dads vs kids" tourney because we're at that cross section where all the dads have played CS2 and now some of the kids are getting old enough and good enough to be competitive.

在 HN 中查看
sonofhans 2026/04/08 03:37
小心。从“我正在向我的孩子展示 CS2”到“我在 CS2 中很容易被我的孩子删除”的交叉速度非常快:)
Careful. The crossover from “I’m showing my kid CS2” to “I’m getting easily deleted by my kid in CS2” is pretty fast :)
在 HN 中查看
freediddy 2026/04/08 04:23
我二十岁出头的侄子在《Madden》中把我打得很惨，以至于我退出了。并不是说他打败了我，我根本无法对抗他得分，而且我认为自己是一名不错的《Madden》玩家。从我给他换尿布、照顾他起，我就一直在玩这个游戏。太丢人了，从那以后我就再也没有碰过这个游戏。
My nephew who is in his early 20s beat me at Madden so badly that I quit. It wasn't that he just beat me, I couldn't score against him at all, and I consider myself a decent Madden player. I've been playing that game since the times I was changing his diapers and babysitting him. It was so humiliating that I haven't touched the game since.
在 HN 中查看
khiguvikohg 2026/04/08 07:23
天哪，如果我见过的话，那是一个严重的危险信号。治疗，伙计。紧急。变得更好。
Christ, that’s some serious red flag if I ever saw one. Therapy, man. Urgently. Get better.
在 HN 中查看
siruwastaken 2026/04/08 03:07
我可能在这里暴露了我的年龄，但是这些电子邮件蠕虫是否由于垃圾邮件过滤而基本上消失了，或者电子邮件程序是否只是获得了更好的病毒防护，从而使其更难以被利用？我今天遇到的唯一电子邮件“病毒”是真实的人类无意中回复“全部回复”合法电子邮件。
I'm probably showing my age here, but did these email worms largely die out due to spam filtering, or did the email programs just get better protections against viruses that made it more difficult to exploit? The only email "viruses" I have come accross today are actual humans accidentally replying "reply all" to a legitimate email.
在 HN 中查看
jprjr_ 2026/04/08 04:26
答案是肯定的，所有这些。

电子邮件提供商拥有更好的垃圾邮件过滤功能，有些提供商对于将任何类型的可执行代码附加到电子邮件有严格的规则（如 - 你就是不能）。

电子邮件客户端总是在更新，在显示内容之前更严格地验证内容等。

The answer is yes, to all of it.

Email providers have better spam filtering, some have strict rules about attaching any kind of executable code to an email (as in - you just can't).

Email clients are always getting updated, stricter about validating content before showing it, etc.

在 HN 中查看
encom 2026/04/08 04:53
互联网也停止使用 Outlook Express。
And the internet stopped using Outlook Express.
在 HN 中查看
khiguvikohg 2026/04/08 07:24
> 我可能在这里暴露了我的年龄，

通过……提出一个诚实的问题，而这个问题绝对不会让提出这个问题的人变老？

来吧，伙计。放弃“灰胡子”行为。

> I'm probably showing my age here,

By… asking an honest question that in no conceivable way ages anyone asking it?

C’mon now, man. Drop the greybeard act.

在 HN 中查看
buserror 2026/04/08 03:33
我被pair.com解雇了，因为我有一封通配符电子邮件，并且正在接收（值得赞扬的是，我丢弃了） 百万 电子邮件...一天...在我的个人域上。哎呀。

25 年后的今天，我仍在使用超级优化的 C++ 电子邮件过滤器。胜过我尝试过的任何其他东西。

I got fired off pair.com because I had a wildcard email, and was receiving (and to my credit, discarding) millions of emails... a day... on my personal domain. Whoops.

I still use my super optimized c++ email filter to this day, 25 years later. Beats anything else I ever tried.

在 HN 中查看

在 HN 中查看
riverforest 2026/04/07 20:34
电子邮件是每个人都抱怨但几乎没有人真正解决的一件事。很好奇这种情况会持续多久才会发生一些重要的事情。
Email is the one thing everyone complains about and almost nobody actually fixes. Curious how long this lasts before something important falls through.
在 HN 中查看
MattTheRealOne 2026/04/07 22:14
电子邮件肯定有其问题，但考虑到所有其他形式的数字通信都变得越来越糟糕并且更加封闭，我不相信替代品会更好。虽然让谷歌和微软等大型提供商接受自托管电子邮件越来越困难，但至少可以选择自己托管一种普遍接受的通信形式仍然很棒。
Email definitely has its issues, but given that every other form of digital communication is getting worse and more locked down, I have no confidence that a replacement would be better. While increasingly difficult to get self-hosted email to be accepted by the big providers like Google and Microsoft, it is still great to at least have the option of hosting a universally accepted form of communication yourself.
在 HN 中查看
BorisMelnik 2026/04/07 22:32
我的理论是，如果 rcs 不断进步，我们可能会在某个时候看到“短信”与电子邮件的合并。

此时，RCS 和电子邮件在纸面上非常相似。

my theory is that if rcs keeps advancing we might see "texting" kind of merge with email at some point.

at this point RCS and email are pretty similar on paper.

在 HN 中查看
WorldMaker 2026/04/08 00:57
RCS 非常集中。超过一半的电话运营商认为运行自己的 RCS 服务器太复杂，因此将其分包给 Google 子公司 (Jibe)。
RCS is incredibly centralized. More than half of the phone carriers decided it was too complicated to run their own RCS servers and so just sub-contract them to a Google subsidiary (Jibe).
在 HN 中查看
jprjr_ 2026/04/08 04:21
公平地说，这似乎是电子邮件正在走下坡路。大多数企业不想运行自己的电子邮件，而只想使用两大提供商之一。
To be fair that seems to be the path email is going down. Most businesses don't want run their own email and just use one of two big providers.
在 HN 中查看
khiguvikohg 2026/04/08 07:26
没有人抱怨电子邮件系统本身，但它是如何被滥用的，而它被滥用只是整个概念的简单和高效的副产品。没有什么可修复的。
No one is complaining about the email system itself, but how it is abused, and it being abused is simply a byproduct of how simple and efficient the whole concept is. There’s nothing to fix.

在 HN 中查看
PurpleRamen 2026/04/07 23:51
几十年来已经有很多修复，但很难改变广泛使用的东西的基本原理。
There have been many fixes over the decades, but it's hard to change the fundamentals of something widely-used.
在 HN 中查看
BadBadJellyBean 2026/04/07 23:16
您对如何修复电子邮件有什么建议吗？

从我的角度来看，所有修复任何问题的尝试都会对较小的发件人造成破坏。今天，如果您想托管邮件服务器，您可以正确设置所有内容（SPF、DKIM、DMARC），但您的电子邮件仍然会进入垃圾邮件文件夹，因为您没有足够的声誉。有整个 IP 地址段被完全禁止参与。

电子邮件被设计为一个分布式系统。这意味着在不破坏大多数系统的情况下无法真正添加新标准。我们仍然没有强制的传输加密。所以我不知道如何解决任何问题，除了改进垃圾邮件过滤并接受它是不完美的。

Do you have any suggestions on how to fix email?

From my perspective all attempts at fixing anything broke something for smaller senders. Today if you want to host a mail server you can set up everything correctly (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) and your email still lands in the spam folder because you have not enough reputation. There are whole IP address segments that a flat out prohibited from participating.

Email is designed to be a distributed system. That means new standards can not really be added without breaking most of the systems. We still don't have mandatory transport encryption. So I don't see how to fix anything but to improve spam filtering and accept that it will be imperfect.

在 HN 中查看
kumardeepanshu 2026/04/08 01:28 deleted
[死的]
[dead]
在 HN 中查看
righthand 2026/04/07 21:25
没有什么可失败的，电子邮件符合它的确切目的。电子邮件应该具有 0 发送/接收摩擦。因此，解决此问题的一种方法是仅接受来自您允许的地址的电子邮件。不过，没有人愿意不断更新他们的地址簿，他们只是想要电子邮件（在收到帐户验证链接后忘记删除营销电子邮件津贴）。那么就没有什么可修复的了。

这种滥用是有意而为之的。

There’s nothing to fall through, email fits it’s exact purpose. Email is supposed to have 0 sending/receiving friction. So one idea to fix it is to only accept email from addresses you’ve allowed. No one wants to constantly update their address book though, they just want the email (forgetting to remove the marketing email allowance after you receive the account verification link). So then there’s nothing to fix.

The abuse is by design.

在 HN 中查看
pizzafeelsright 2026/04/08 01:46
通过避免使用电子邮件来修复。 我只检查电子邮件以获取密码重置和银行通知。我从不发送电子邮件。 与工作之外的任何人沟通的所有其他渠道都是短信。
Email is fixed by avoiding the usage. I only check my email for password resets and bank notifications. I never send email. Every other channel of communication with anyone outside of work is a text message.
在 HN 中查看
